title: Median Wage Impact from AI by 2030
description: Projected change in real median wages in the US due to AI adoption, combining productivity gains and displacement effects.

overlays.label: Acemoglu & Johnson: shared prosperity requires deliberate policy choices
Otis et al.: No avg AI effect on Kenyan SME revenue; high performers +15%, low -8%
UPS: 12K management jobs cut; driver top rate $49/hr under new contract
Indeed: AI-exposed wages grew 1.2% vs 4.1% non-exposed
Walmart: investing in checkout/inventory automation; min wage $14/hr
QJE: AI assistance increases worker productivity by 15% on average
Korinek & Stiglitz: wage growth requires capital-augmenting AI
Goldman Sachs: AI to raise labor productivity ~15% when fully adopted
Autor (NBER): Falling labor share drove 16% of US real wage growth; automation paradoxically raises wages
Autor (NBER): Further automation should raise wages in all 12 industrialized countries studied
Stanford: small positive wage effects in AI-exposed jobs
Jones & Tonetti (Stanford/NBER): AI output gains modest near-term — only 4% above trend by 2040
Althoff & Reichardt model: +21% long-run average wage increase from AI productivity
ICLE: small but significant positive wage effects in AI-exposed occupations
CEPR/BIS: AI boosts EU firm productivity 4%; higher wages per employee
Dallas Fed: AI exposure has ~zero effect on wages at median experience premium (CI includes 0)
Furman (BLS): productivity 2.2% above CBO forecast; 2.8% annual growth

sources.title: AI and the US Labor Market
Global Wage Report 2022-23: The impact of inflation and COVID-19 on wages and purchasing power
AI Will Transform the Global Economy. Let's Make Sure It Benefits Humanity
sources.excerpt: In most scenarios, AI will likely worsen overall inequality. Higher-income workers may see disproportionate wage gains.

sources.title: The Impact of Artificial Intelligence on the Labor Market
sources.excerpt: AI exposure is concentrated among high-skill, high-wage occupations, but wage effects may cascade downward.

sources.title: AI could be coming for higher-paid workers this time
Employment Cost Index — 2025 Q1
The Wage Compression that Persisted
State of Working America: AI and Wage Trends
Walmart Lowers Starting Pay for New Hires
sources.excerpt: Walmart's minimum starting wage is $14/hr while average U.S. associate wage reached $18.25 by 2025. The company invested in checkout and inventory automation but has not disclosed specific per-location staffing reduction figures.

sources.title: US Wage Tracker — Posted Wage Trends by Occupation
sources.excerpt: Posted wages for AI-exposed occupations (admin, data entry, basic accounting) grew 1.2% YoY vs. 4.1% for non-AI-exposed roles, suggesting wage suppression.

sources.title: UPS cuts 12,000 jobs, citing softer demand and higher union labor costs
sources.excerpt: UPS announced 12,000 job cuts, primarily management and salaried positions, as part of a $1 billion cost-reduction effort. Full-time driver pay raised to $49/hr top rate under 2023 Teamsters contract.

sources.title: PwC Global AI Jobs Barometer 2025
sources.excerpt: AI-skilled workers command a 56% wage premium, up from 25% in prior year. AI roles median compensation at $157K vs. $49,500 all-occupation median.

sources.title: BLS Occupational Employment and Wage Statistics, May 2024
sources.excerpt: Median annual wage for all occupations: $49,500. AI-exposed administrative and clerical wages grew 1.1% vs. 3.8% for non-AI-exposed occupations.

sources.title: Economic Growth under Transformative AI
sources.publisher: Annual Review of Economics / NBER (Korinek, Trammell)
sources.excerpt: Sufficiently advanced AI could dramatically raise growth rates and lower labor share, breaking Kaldor Facts. Wages may rise or fall depending on returns to scale and direction of technical change.

sources.title: Power and Progress: Our Thousand-Year Struggle Over Technology and Prosperity
sources.excerpt: Technology does not automatically benefit workers; shared prosperity requires deliberate policy choices. AI could follow historical pattern of concentrating gains among capital owners.

sources.title: Steering Technological Progress
sources.excerpt: For the empirically plausible case that capital and labor are gross complements, raising wages requires capital-augmenting innovation rather than labor-augmenting.

sources.title: Generative AI at Work
sources.excerpt: Access to AI assistance increases worker productivity, as measured by issues resolved per hour, by 15% on average, with substantial heterogeneity across workers.

sources.title: AI, Productivity, and Labor Markets: A Review of the Empirical Evidence
sources.excerpt: Small but statistically significant positive wage effects found across AI-exposed occupations; 35.9% of US workers used generative AI by December 2025.

sources.title: Task-Specific Technical Change and Comparative Advantage
sources.excerpt: raising average wages by 21 percent

sources.title: Labor Market Effects of Generative AI
sources.excerpt: Small positive wage effects identified for workers in AI-exposed occupations; adoption concentrated among younger, college-educated, higher-earning employees.

sources.title: How AI is affecting productivity and jobs in Europe
sources.publisher: CEPR VoxEU (Aldasoro, Gambacorta, Pál, Revoltella, Weiss, Wolski)
sources.excerpt: AI adoption causally increases labour productivity levels by 4% on average. Employees in AI-using companies benefit through higher wages, both in total and per employee.

sources.title: AI is simultaneously aiding and replacing workers, wage data suggest
sources.publisher: Federal Reserve Bank of Dallas (J. Scott Davis)
sources.excerpt: For occupations with the median experience premium (40 percent), a one-standard-deviation increase in AI exposure is associated with a 0.05 percentage point decline in wage growth.

sources.title: Past Automation and Future A.I.: How Weak Links Tame the Growth Explosion
sources.excerpt: Despite the accelerating growth, the effects of A.I. on GDP per person are remarkably small for the next 20 to 40 years.

sources.title: Nonfarm Business Sector Labor Productivity: 2.2% Above CBO Pre-Pandemic Forecast
sources.excerpt: Are we finally seeing AI in the productivity data? A big upward revision to earlier data and strong Q4 bring us 2.2% above CBO's pre-pandemic forecast. Annual rates: 1 year: 2.8%, 2 years: 2.5%, 6 years: 2.2%

sources.title: The Uneven Impact of Generative AI on Entrepreneurial Performance
sources.publisher: HBS Working Paper (Otis, Clarke, Delecourt, Holtz, Koning)
sources.excerpt: The paper reports no statistically significant average effect on revenues or profits. But effects are highly heterogeneous: high‑performing businesses at baseline appear to improve (roughly 15 percent), while low performers do worse (roughly 8-10 percent worse)

sources.title: Resolving the Automation Paradox: Falling Labor Share, Rising Wages
sources.excerpt: falling labor share accounted for 16% of U.S. real wage growth between 1954 and 2019
